title: Melee Markets
summary: Melee is a Solana prediction market positioned as 'Pump.fun meets Polymarket.' Arcium currently presents it as one of the verified projects coming to mainnet, but public docs on the Arcium side are still thin.
description:
Melee markets itself as a viral prediction market built on Solana with an early-access product. The clearest current public description comes from the official site, which frames the product as a bet-on-anything market designed for fast, social trading behavior.

On the Arcium side, Melee appears on the mainnet page as a verified project coming to Arcium. That is enough to treat it as part of the ecosystem, but not enough to describe a confirmed Arcium integration pattern yet.

bodySections.title: What_Atlas_Reviewed
bodySections.body: The official site presents Melee as a Solana prediction market with early-access positioning and a clear consumer angle. The product language is intentionally simple: viral markets, bet on anything, and a hybrid of Pump.fun's social energy with Polymarket's market format.

bodySections.title: Arcium_Relevance
bodySections.body: Arcium's mainnet page lists Melee among verified projects coming to the network and describes it as Pump.fun meets Polymarket. That is meaningful ecosystem evidence, but I did not find a current Melee-published source describing how Arcium is used inside the product.

statusNote: Arcium lists Melee as a verified project coming to mainnet, but public material does not yet document a specific Arcium-powered feature or workflow.

sources.label: Melee Website
sources.note: Official product site for Melee's Solana prediction market.

sources.label: Melee App
sources.note: Early-access app currently live under the alpha subdomain.

sources.note: Arcium lists Melee among verified projects coming to the network.
